_about: ABP textual-variant footnotes (the dagger apparatus from the ABP 2nd ed.). Shape: book -> chapter -> verse -> [ {src, op, gloss, conf} ]. src = printed Greek edition the variant comes from; op = adds/reads/omits; gloss = the English meaning of the variant word (the Greek form is omitted — it is unrecoverable as clean text from the source). conf = how the verse anchor was found (auto = exact text match; ai = meaning-matched). FILTERED: a note is only kept when its English actually differs from our ABP text — variants that read the same in English (a Greek-only or trivial difference, e.g. 'part' vs 'parts') are dropped, since they look redundant to a reader. Isaiah only for now (proof).
